Would you rather: random(360) vs. random(180)*2
colonel type barrage fight, bullets coming from top middle
you would just lose half precision
i wonder at what point you would be able to see a noticeable difference
it's complicated maybe you don't lose precision after all? because like between 0 and 1 you have more numbers
I'd think it still loses precision because game maker can only go up to a certain decimal point
yeah I have no idea what gamemaker does
I want to add that I think it's fucked up people use degrees for angles
radians are also kind of trash
the best angle unit is turns I don't know why no one uses them
your right. everyone should have a copypaste for pi at all times when coding
with turns you don't need any of that
its not like turns are any better
that is better than 90 or half pi
and more specifically, Would you rather: random(360) vs. random(180)*2
what are your thoughts on this boss so far?
if you were dodging rng from a boss, colonel style, single spawner
i like it a lot as well, its really barrage heavy
and the random attacks are fun
what is your opinion on angle units amealiandyou
its just notations that are probably made for different uses, and turns are probably just as ignorant for other use cases
its probably fine for the most basic of things
turns are better are every gamemaker use case come on
@lechurrajo i like degrees if i have to choose ;-;
but usually they want pi stuff
is there a point where there would be plasma?
like, is random(360) identical to random(1)*360
@ameliandyou like its annoying to write out PI * (2/3) as opposed to writing the angle
it's half the angle it's confusing
turns are the better option you know I'm right
you got me reading on the origins of this and honestly it all seems fine
also I am on this boss too
@lechurrajo tau is a built in constant in godot, i guess that is essentially turns correct?
but with turns you skip multiplying by tau
how can you defend the method that adds another step come on
ive been stuck on this boss for 3 hours already T.T
no you have convinced me lechurrajo
when would you care about tau
i am fine with things having extra steps that relate them to the point of what you are doing
you see amelia. tau is very literally 2 pi
how does tau relate it to something
it’s so luckbased, the attack transitions just decide to kill you so often
i wish pickle rika was here
like I know tau is the length of the perimeter of a circle of lenght 1 but why would I care about that
@raker67 i mainly brought it up because i thought it was what lechurrajo was referring to
the step you are skipping is basically not being literal math which is fine but the way you talk about it is like
ah, through the horrible platform thing
if i started counting with my hands and then i complained about axioms
like bro who cares about it
but the angle unit is not a math axion is a choice
you could use degrees if you wanted
but they add steps why would you want them
ok but who Cares in my context that im discussing heehee
the planet revolves around my use cases after All
but for most use cases turns are better come on
well yea thats what im saying
im not saying lechurrajo has to write pi on everything, its just like the most noneburger complaint ever
minimizing the importance of angle unit choice...
its like complaining about the decimal system while im asking how many apples are in my basket
if math didn't exist you would never play fangames
guy who is into precision btw

my credentials: got an A+ in Calc III
also you get more precision if you use turns because there are more numbers between 0 and 1
